title: First Great Schism

{{Quote|The Jedi called it the Great Schism-as tends to happen, later events forced it to be renamed the First Great Schism.|[[Arden Lyn]]|<ref name="EGW" />}}
The '''First Great Schism''' was the first of the [[Great Schism/Legends|Great Schisms]] between followers of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and those who refused to follow the Jedi hierarchy's dictates. Its contemporaries simply called it the '''Great Schism''', which it remained known as until the [[Second Great Schism]] necessitated referring to the initial conflict as the First.

==Origins==
{{Quote|It seems too easy to blame everything on Xendor and the dark side. Maybe we… maybe the Jedi were partly to blame, too.|[[Danzigorro Potts]]|<ref name="JedivsSith" />}}
For over ten millennia, a society of Force-wielders called the [[Je'daii Order]] had lived in relative peace on the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] world of [[Tython/Legends|Tython]],<ref name="SWDotJ1">[[Dawn of the Jedi: Force Storm 1|''Dawn of the Jedi: Force Storm'' 1]]</ref> when some of them relocated in order to step outside the growing sphere of influence of the fledgling [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and founded the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. They made their new home on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].<ref name="EGW" />

Already at this point, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] had grown doctrinaire in its practices and, according to some, too rigid to allow growth. Such an opinion was held by the [[Kashi Mer Dynasty|Kashi Mer]] Jedi Knight [[Xendor]],<ref name="EGW" /> who was exiled from his homeworld for embracing the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|darker aspects of the Force]]<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|III|341|Xendor}}</ref> and wished to study the Bogan more. He had grown vocal in his criticism of what he perceived as the Jedi Order's increasingly exclusionary ways about using the Force, and petitioned the leaders of the Jedi Order to be allowed to depart Ossus in order to create a new academy. There, he hoped to teach and study alternative traditions of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], such as [[Order of Dai Bendu|Dai Bendu]], the [[Followers of Palawa|Palawa]], the [[Way of the Dark]] and the [[Protectorate of the Hidden]], to name just a few. The leaders of the Jedi Order refused Xendor's request, but he exited in defiance despite their decision. He found a supporter in a young [[Steel Hand of Palawa]] named [[Arden Lyn]] who shared his views and was dissatisfied with the Jedi Order embracing the philosophy of the [[Caamasi/Legends|Caamasi]] people.<ref name="EGW" />

==Schism==
===A new academy===
Xendor and Lyn departed Ossus to establish a [[Legionnaire Academy|new academy]] on [[Lettow]]. They were not the only ones to defy the dictates of the Jedi Order; increasingly larger groups left Ossus to join Xendor's new group and study mysteries of the Force that had been forbidden to them under the Jedi Order. Eventually, Xendor's followers came to be known as the [[Legions of Lettow]].<ref name="EGW" />

The newcomers to Xendor's academy pledged their allegiance to him and to the protection of their new order. They swore to defend their right to pursue knowledge of the Force without the oversight of the Jedi hierarchs, deeming them unfit to dictate which avenues were correct and which were forbidden.<ref name="EGW" /> Members of the Legion embraced the dark side of the Force, rejected the idea of order and structure and celebrated their freedom by giving in their passions and emotions and touching the power of the dark side.<ref name="EGW" /><ref name="JedivsSith" />

===Open war===
Sources are divided on which side started the war. According to some historians, it was the rageful Xendor and his army who attacked the Jedi Order,<ref name="TOR">{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 13: The First Great Schism}}</ref> while others, including Arden Lyn, say it was the Jedi who declared war against the Legions and raised an army first.<ref name="JedivsSith" /><ref name="EGW" />

One of the first [[Battle of Ossus (First Great Schism)|battles]] was fought on the Jedi capital world Ossus. Claiming to wish to end the conflict early and prevent it from spilling into the Republic, Xendor led the Legions in an attack on Ossus. Their attack failed, and the war continued unabated.<ref name="EGW" />

Lyn claimed, millennia later, that Xendor tried to alert the young Republic to the threat he believed the Jedi posed to them: that they would guise themselves as protectors and guardians while they secretly schemed for power. In this, too, Xendor failed, and the Legions failed to gain any traction in the Republic against their Jedi enemies.<ref name="EGW" />

The conflict spread across a number of worlds as it intensified. War spread to [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]], [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal]], the Republic capital [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and [[Metellos/Legends|Metellos]] as well, before a significant [[Battle of Columus|battle]] on [[Columus/Legends|Columus]]. At Columus, Xendor engaged the Jedi [[Awdrysta Pina]], known by his nickname Green Blade, and was slain in single combat.<ref name="EGW" />

Lyn would later muse that both she and Xendor had admired Pina as a free thinker, one who preferred to travel to unknown places in search of mysteries of the Force unknown to the Jedi Order. Ultimately, however, Pina believed that the order and structure were the foundations of civilization, so he stood with the Jedi rather than with Xendor.<ref name="EGW" />

The Jedi forces commanded by Pina held a unique advantage over the Legions which even Lyn was forced to admit: their dedication to order allowed them to subsume their individuality into a larger collective and fight as one—not unlike the Force ability known as [[Force-meld|battle-meld]]. Xendor's followers refused to embrace any such tactic, believing it insectile and an affront to individuality.<ref name="EGW" />

===Extermination of the Legions===
Victory over Xendor, however, was not sufficient for the Jedi to declare victory in the war. Instead, forces under Pina were sent to Lettow to attempt to exterminate the last of Xendor's followers. A fierce [[Battle of Lettow|battle]] was fought, allowing just enough time for the last survivors of the Legions to flee beyond the boundaries of Republic space.<ref name="EGW" />

Lyn was eventually cornered by Pina on [[Irkalla]], after fleeing into regions of space unknown to all but Xendor. The two engaged in a [[Duel on Irkalla|duel]] in which she was disarmed. Unwilling to concede defeat, she channeled her strongest emotions—rage and sorrow, love for Xendor, and despair over what she believed had been unfairly taken from them—through the [[Kashi Mer talisman]], destroying Pina's sword and mortally wounding him in the process.<ref name="EGW" />

In his dying breaths, he drew on his own unique Force talent, [[Morichro/Legends|Morichro]], to defeat Lyn. The spell, in essence, placed her into a deep stasis. She would not be resuscitated until the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]], [[4 BBY/Legends|24,496 years later]].<ref name="EGW" />

==Aftermath==
The Jedi Order withdrew to Ossus in the Great Schism's aftermath. Thereafter, few in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] gave the Jedi much thought, though unbeknownst to them, the Jedi acted as the Republic's secret defenders on the Rim, working quietly to check the ambitions of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] towards vulnerable colonies in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]], and keeping the militaristic states of the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]] divided. They did not declare themselves to the Republic again until the [[Tionese War]], nearly 500 years later.<ref name="EGW" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] book [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi -'' ''The Golden Age of the Sith'' #0]], published on [[July 31]], [[1996]]<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54589/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_golden_age_of_the_sith_1996|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Golden Age of the Sith (1996)}}</ref> by [[Dark Horse Comics]],<ref name="DarkHorse">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/95-416/Star-Wars-Tales-of-the-Jedi---The-Golden-Age-of-the-Sith-0-of-5|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Golden Age of the Sith #0 (of 5)}}</ref> a young [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi]], [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]], conveys tales of the Sith to [[Ooroo/Legends|Master Ooroo]], recounting that "[[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]" of "the First Great Schism of the Jedi Knights" were "defeated after a century of bloodshed" and that the "[[Exiles|Jedi Exiles]]", noted as such in the text of issue #0, subsequently "forged the [[Sith Empire (Pre-Republic)|Sith civilization]] into a [[Sith Empire/Legends|new empire]] ".<ref name="GAS0">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 0]]</ref> In [[1996]]'s [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi'' – ''The Golden Age of the Sith'' #2]], the [[Force ghost/Legends|ghost]] of [[Marka Ragnos]] recounted how this "Great Schism between the Dark Side and the Light turned Jedi against Jedi."<ref name="TOTJ 2">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]]</ref> 

"[[The Emperor's Pawns]]", a collection of biographies written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and published in [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' #5]] ([[2001]]), implied that [[Xendor]] and his lover [[Arden Lyn]] started the First Great Schism some "250 centuries" prior to her reawakening,<ref name="tep">{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}}</ref> which would place it circa [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]]. On [[June 29]], [[2006]], Abel revealed that he had exorcised portions of his article, "[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]" related to a Second Great Schism involving the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerers of Tund]] in [[10,000 BBY]].<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24255327|board=Literature|thread=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|PTtime=2006-06-29|quote=...I never updated the Sorcerers of Tund section of Evil Never Dies (which originally had them as a group of Jedi schismatics of an intellectually rather than violently motivated Second Great Schism, placed around 10,000 BBY)...|archiveurl=https://boards.theforce.net/threads/evil-never-dies-the-sith-dynasties.24202765/page-9#post-24255327}}</ref>

The lore about the First Great Schism from [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 0|''Tales of the Jedi'' – ''The Golden Age of the Sith'' #0]] and the date from ''[[The Emperor's Pawns]]'' were restated by the timeline at the beginning of ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' ([[2002]]) as well as in the beginning of [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] entry.''<ref name="NEGC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> 

''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ([[2005]]) revealed that the [[Exiles|Jedi Exiles]] lived around ~7000 BBY, along with the fact that the "century of bloodshed" mentioned in ''Tales of the Jedi: Golden Age of the Sith'' is the same event as the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]] (said to be a separate event in the timeline in the beginning of The New Essential Guide to Characters) and that it followed a "[[Second Great Schism]]", while the "First Great Schism" was [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] to be a separate event.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> In spite of this retcon, ''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Special Edition]]'', which was published in [[May]] [[2007]],<ref name="SDate">{{DK|host=dorlingkindersley-uk.co.uk|url=nf/Book/BookDisplay/0,,9781405318686,00.html|text=Star Wars the Ultimate Visual Guide|nolive=1}}</ref> and ''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'', which was published on [[April 30]], [[2012]],<ref name="UDate">{{DK|subdomain=us|url=nf/Book/BookDisplay/0,,9780756692483,00.html|text=Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded|nolive=1}}</ref> say that the Exiles turned to the dark side "in the early years of the Old Republic."<ref name="UVGSE">''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Special Edition]]''</ref><ref name="UVGUE">''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]''</ref>

When asked about this retcon, [[Daniel Wallace]], author of ''The New Esssential Chronology'', said it was because of "LFL internal documents" dating Dark Jedi contact with the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]] at ~7000 BBY.<ref name="NECt">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2005/10/new-essential-chronology-talkback.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=The New Essential Chronology: Talkback thread}}</ref> On [[January 11]], 2006, [[Leland Chee]] said that this retcon was to "correct the the [sic] original discrepency of the timing of the Great Schism introduced by ''The New Essential Guide to Characters''."<ref name="Leland Chee">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=630|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-01-11|time=04:12 PM}}</ref> On [[June 30]], 2006, Abel G. Peña stated that he "didn't quite understand this realignment of the origins of the Sith."<ref name="JCFAbel">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24263085|board=Literature|thread=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|PTtime=2006-06-29|quote=I didn't quite understand this realignment of the origins of the Sith either, but at least for now it is what it is.|archiveurl=https://boards.theforce.net/threads/evil-never-dies-the-sith-dynasties.24202765/page-10#post-24263085}}</ref>
